stories.title: Thursday briefing: ​What difference will the ceasefire in the Middle East make, and will it hold?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: The truce offers a reprieve after weeks of turmoil, ​b​ut unresolved disputes and competing interpretations ​of what was agreed, threaten to pull the region back toward crisis​ at a moment’s notice Good morning. On Tuesday, just an hour before the deadline imposed by Donald Trump for Iran to reopen navigation in the strait of Hormuz or face a wave of “civilisation-ending” strikes, a two-week pause in hostilities was announced. stories.title: Brussels backs windfall tax on big firms to ease pain from energy crisis
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission’s economy chief on Thursday gave his blessing to EU countries seeking to introduce a windfall tax on energy companies that are profiting from skyrocketing fuel prices. “There is nothing preventing member states from applying this,” Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis told MEPs during a hearing at the European Parliament in Brussels. “We are looking into whether we can come up with some kind of more coordinated approach at the European level. It’

stories.title: Trump hurt Nato’s credibility more in past weeks than Putin managed in years, says Czech president – Europe live
stories.summary: Petr Pavel, a former senior Nato general, says it is unfair to expect defensive alliance to ‘automatically help in wars waged outside its territory’ Donald Trump has taken to his Truth Social platform again on Thursday to renew his criticism of the alliance. The US president posted that “none of these people” (which people is unclear), including “our own, very disappointing Nato, understood anything unless they have pressure placed upon them!!!”. stories.title: German Bundestag chief visits Baltic states to discuss cyber defence on NATO's eastern flank
stories.summary: Klöckner will also visit the German Bundeswehr brigade in Lithuania on Thursday. Around 5,000 German soldiers are to be deployed to Lithuania by 2027 to support the local military.

stories.title: Hungary betrayed EU by revealing sanctions talk to Russia, says French top diplomat
stories.summary: PARIS — Hungary’s talks with Russian officials about sanctions were “a betrayal of the solidarity that is required among members of the European Union,” France’s Foreign Affairs Minister Jean-Noël Barrot said in an interview Thursday. Late last month, Hungary’s top diplomat Péter Szijjártó admitted he had spoken with Russian officials “on sanctions-related matters” as the EU considered new economic measures targeting Moscow. stories.title: Most Hungarians want better relations with EU after years of Orban Brussels-bashing, poll finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Survey published days before election in which anti-EU prime minister risks being ousted after 16 years Europe live – latest updates Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? After years of relentless EU-bashing by their nationalist, illiberal prime minister, an overwhelming majority of Hungary’s voters back its membership of the bloc, and most – including many of Viktor Orbán’s voters – now want a new approach to Brussels. Days before elections at which Orbán, who

stories.title: Poland's Catholic Church prepares parishes as crisis centres amid war fears
stories.summary: The Polish Bishops' Conference has established a special working group to develop an action plan in the event of an armed conflict reaching Poland, as "most Poles will first turn to the church for help, and only then to municipal institutions and offices," officials say.

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: „nem hanyatlottunk a csicskák nívójára le, nem tagozódtunk egy hitvány szisztémába be − ja: de”

stories.summary: A legtöbb érvényes szavazási iratot olyan választók juttatták vissza, akik romániai értesítési címet adtak meg a névjegyzékbe vételükről szóló értesítéshez.

stories.title: Politológus: A Fidesznek nem a szavazatgyűjtés a célja Erdélyben, hanem a függési viszony megerősítése
stories.summary: Toró Tibor szerint továbbra sem látszik az erdélyi elit B terve kormányváltás esetére, ráadásul a Fidesz mindent megtesz annak érdekében, hogy megnehezítse a „kiugrást”. Orbánék európai különutassága gyengítheti az RMDSZ romániai pozícióját, és ezt az erdélyi közösség is megérezheti.

stories.title: A Mol 500 millió dollárnyi olajat vesz az USA-tól, a kormány pedig HIMARS rakétarendszereket vásárol

A Tisza Pártnak kétharmados győzelmet becslő Mediánt hamisítással vádolta, szerinte a kormánypártoknak jó esélyük van a többség megszerzésére. A választás előtti utolsó kormányinfón a Telex zsinórban negyedszerre nem kérdezhetett.

stories.title: Germans pining for Gerhard Schröder forget his errors and ties to Putin. The SPD needs a fresh approach | Katja Hoyer
stories.summary: The former chancellor was the chief architect of Germany’s dependence on Russian fossil fuels and cuts to defence spending. Both haunt the country today The former German chancellor, Gerhard Schröder, is enjoying a curious political revival. Not so long ago, his reputation seemed in tatters. In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Ireland deploys army to clear protests blocking key ports
stories.summary: DUBLIN — The Irish government deployed the army Thursday to remove trucks and tractors currently blocking key ports used for importing fuel and critical chemicals into the country. Justice Minister Jim O’Callaghan announced the move following two days of round-the-clock blockades by hauliers and farmers angry over the surging price of motor fuel. The grassroots and apparently leaderless protesters, who are coordinating their actions on social media platforms, say they’ll keep snarling key roads

stories.summary: PARIS — French MEP Rima Hassan has been cleared of wrongdoing after being accused of carrying illegal drugs, according to a statement from her party and the Paris prosecutor’s office. “Scientific analyses have found no traces of synthetic drugs in the items seized from Rima Hassan,” the hard-left political party to which she belongs, France Unbowed, said in a statement Thursday. stories.title: Gaza mourns Al Jazeera reporter Mohammed Wishah killed iin Israeli drone strike
stories.summary: Gaza was mourning Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah on Thursday after he was killed in an Israeli drone strike that hit his car west of Gaza City a day earlier.

stories.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
stories.summary: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK rejects tolls on Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain’s top diplomat on Thursday rejected Iran’s push to charge ships to travel through the Strait of Hormuz. Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper said the vital waterway is an international shipping and transit route which means the freedom of navigation principles apply. “Countries cannot simply hijack those kinds of international transit routes and unilaterally apply tolls,” Cooper told the BBC. “They cannot do that as part of the laws of the sea and the United Nations arran

stories.title: EU’s Kallas urges ‘heavy-handed’ Israel to end strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas urged Israel to halt its attacks in Lebanon, which she said went beyond self-defense against Hezbollah and endangered the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement. “Hezbollah dragged Lebanon into the war, but Israel’s right to defend itself does not justify inflicting such massive destruction,” Kallas wrote in a post on X . “Israeli strikes killed hundreds last night, making it hard to argue such heavy-handed actions fall within self-defense.” The criticism comes after L

stories.title: Italy’s Meloni pivots away from Trump as she looks to reset her premiership
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni distanced herself from U.S. President Donald Trump during a parliamentary address on Thursday, in a bid to save her premiership after a bruising referendum defeat last month. “The West stands on two legs: the European and the American one. If they don’t move in the same direction, it risks paralysis,” Meloni warned, adding that she continued “to believe in the need to work to ensure the unity of the West.” The speech comes after the g

stories.title: Russian GRU exploited vulnerable routers worldwide to steal sensitive information, probe reveals
stories.summary: An international investigation revealed how Russia’s GRU cyber unit, known as "Fancy Bear," managed to steal sensitive information from governments and the military using badly protected routers.

stories.title: Hundreds search for wolf that escaped from zoo in South Korea
stories.summary: Local school closes in Daejeon city as hundreds of emergency service and military personnel scour area around O-World theme park where the wolf escaped from Authorities are hunting for a wolf after it escaped from a zoo in Daejeon, a South Korean city with a population of 1.5million. More than 300 people – including firefighters, police and military personnel – are taking part in the search operation, an official from the Daejeon fire headquarters said. Continue reading...

She has lost 20lbs, and wakes up every day with a headache Each day in detention feels like 48 hours for Olivia. stories.title: Argentina approves Milei’s glacier mining bill amid environmental protests
stories.summary: Legislative change backed by libertarian president makes it easier to extract metals in frozen parts of the Andes Argentina’s congress has approved a bill promoted by the libertarian president, Javier Milei, that authorises mining in ecologically sensitive areas of glaciers and permafrost, outraging environmentalists. stories.title: OpenAI pulls out of landmark £31bn UK investment package

Stargate UK was a part of the landmark UK-US AI deal announced last September , in which US companies appeared to commit £31bn to the UK’s tech sector, part of a larger series of investments intended to “mainline AI” into the British economy. stories.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife found guilty of attempted manslaughter
stories.summary: Prosecutors alleged Gerhardt Konig, 47, had planned to kill Arielle Konig during a birthday trip to Honolulu A Hawaii anesthesiologist who was accused of trying to murder his wife on a cliffside hike last year has been convicted of attempted manslaughter, a lesser charge. A Honolulu jury returned the verdict against Gerhardt Konig, 47, on Wednesday after a day of deliberations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: Woman who allegedly attacked Sydney hospital patient with hammer claims he stole her brother’s ashes, court hears
stories.summary: Woman, 46, charged with grievous bodily harm after she allegedly struck 63-year-old RPA patient in head, NSW police say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man is fighting for his life after being allegedly attacked with a hammer at a Sydney hospital by a woman he knew who claimed he had stolen her brother’s ashes, a court has heard. Viki Graham, 46, was refused bail and will spend at least two months in jail after

stories.title: Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China
stories.summary: Beijing’s powerbrokers are credited with winning Iran over, although one analyst says they were ‘pushing an open door’ Middle East crisis – live updates As the world struggles to make sense of what, if anything, was achieved by the ceasefire deal announced by the US and Iran on Tuesday, one major power that stands to win regardless is China. Beijing’s powerbrokers are being credited with pushing Iran towards agreeing to the ceasefire, bolstering its status as a regional mediator. In China’s tigh

stories.title: Bitten by snakes 200 times – on purpose: US man’s quest to help deliver new antivenom
stories.summary: Tim Friede put his ‘ass on the line’ to help stop snakebite deaths – whose numbers appear to be rising amid the climate crisis As we overheat and degrade our planet, more people are set to come into contact, sometimes fatally, with venomous snakes. One man hopes to provide an unusual solution to this, after subjecting himself to 200 intentional snakebites to his body. For nearly 20 years, Tim Friede, 58, allowed some of the most lethal snakes in the world to bite him so he could build up an immu

stories.title: Ukraine ceasefire back in focus as US security guarantees fade and casualties mount
stories.summary: Divisions between Washington and European capitals over Iran fuel concerns about US commitment to peace deal As a ceasefire was declared in the Middle East, Volodymyr Zelenskyy sought to draw attention to the war in his own country, posting on social media that Ukraine had consistently pushed for a ceasefire in the war “being waged by Russia here, in Europe”. Efforts to end the war in Ukraine have largely stalled since the Iran war began, with trilateral talks between Kyiv, Moscow and Washington

stories.title: Four people die in Channel small-boat sinking
stories.summary: At least 42 others rescued after incident in strong currents off coast of Boulogne Two men and two women have died after a small boat sank in the Channel between France and Britain, French local authorities have said. They died after being swept away by strong currents while trying to board a dinghy, according to François-Xavier Lauch, the prefect of Pas-de-Calais. The dinghy was described as a taxi-boat, which travels along stretches of the northern French and Belgian coasts, picking up refugee

stories.title: London pub thief sold £2.2m Fabergé egg and watch set to buy drugs
stories.summary: Enzo Conticello jailed for 27 months for ‘opportunistic’ theft of Givenchy handbag containing precious jewels A man who stole a handbag containing a Fabergé egg and watch set valued at up to £2.2m has been jailed for more than two years. Enzo Conticello, 29, took the Givenchy bag belonging to Rosie Dawson as she stood in the smoking area of the Dog and Duck pub in Soho, London, on 7 November 2024. Continue reading...

stories.title: BA to reduce Middle East flights when services resume in July
stories.summary: After suspending routes owing to Iran war, airline will operate more direct flights to India and Kenya Middle East crisis – live updates British Airways will offer a reduced flight schedule to the Middle East when it resumes services in July, and use the aircraft to operate more direct flights to India and Kenya. The airline has currently suspended services to the region because of the Iran war , and plans to resume flights to Saudi Arabia’s capital, Riyadh, in mid-May, as well as services to Du

stories.title: Cost of living and mental health crisis driving mass animal rescues, says RSPCA
stories.summary: Charity says it dealt with 75 incidents last year involving 100 or more animals living in one property The cost of living crisis and an increase in people experiencing mental health difficulties have led to a rising number of multi-animal rescues in England and Wales, an RSPCA superintendent has said. The animal charity this week had to confirm that a shocking photograph of more than 250 poodle-cross dogs found at a property in the UK was not faked with artificial intelligence. The RSPCA took in

stories.title: Lidl to open 50 UK stores in year ahead as part of £600m expansion plans
stories.summary: Almost 2,000 jobs will be created, with retailer vying to overtake Morrisons as Britain’s fifth largest supermarket Lidl is to open 50 new UK stores in the year ahead as it aims to overtake Morrisons as the country’s fifth largest supermarket chain. The German-owned retailer, which now has more than 1,000 British stores, said it planned to invest more than £600m in UK growth, creating almost 2,000 jobs as it expands its warehouse and logistic network to supply its new outlets. Continue reading..

stories.title: ‘There’s no Hezbollah here’: the Lebanese reaction to Israeli strikes that killed hundreds
stories.summary: Beirut residents and officials say thousand-pound-bombs mainly hit civilians in mission dubbed ‘Operation Eternal Darkness’ Middle East crisis – live updates It took Israel only 10 minutes to carry out one of the worst mass-killings in Lebanon since the end of the country’s civil war in 1990. Omar Rakha heard the war planes but did not feel the explosions; it was only when he woke up face down on the street, bleeding, that he understood what had happened. The building next to his in the Barbour

stories.title: ‘The danger and value of water are in my blood’: how rain fences are making Dutch homes more climate resilient
stories.summary: Housing corporations are adopting rainwater storage in garden fences, reducing pressure during downpours and preserving water for times of drought Good fences make good neighbours – but rain fences could make even better ones. That is the hope of housing corporations in the Netherlands, which are adopting rainwater storage in their garden fences. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Jasveen Sangha, a British-American dual citizen, had pleaded guilty to supplying the "Friends" star with the drugs that led to the actor's death in 2023.

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95

stories.summary: The four astronauts said they were returning to Earth with "so many more pictures, so many more stories".

stories.summary: Bali police chief Daniel Adityajaya said Lyons was wanted in Spain as the alleged "leader of a large-scale transnational criminal organisation" engaged in drug trafficking and money laundering.

Ein Thema dürften die Kontroversen zwischen der EU und den USA über Technologie-Themen sein. „Wir sind offen dafür, Missverständnisse mit den USA zu klären“, sagte der Sprecher der Kommission für Technologie, Thomas Regnier, am Dienstag. Die EU hat sich dem Druck der USA widersetzt, Vorschriften für digitale Märkte zu locke

stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody

After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: Iran doubles down on executions
stories.summary: Iran has executed 14 individuals in recent weeks, with the hangings continuing unabated regardless of the US-Israeli military campaign against the country.

stories.title: Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
stories.summary: Authorities in Nigeria's restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.

stories.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar

stories.summary: The latest strike to hit Germany's flagship carrier hails from the cabin crew union UFO. Meanwhile, Berlin is walking back a contentious plan about young men seeking permission to go abroad for long periods.

stories.summary: Very few people are ever prosecuted following terror attacks with suspects often held without trial.

stories.title: Digital detox: Offline club events in Netherlands offer screen-free social connection
stories.summary: Participants in Amsterdam and Utrecht join “offline club” events, handing in their phones to spend time on creative activities and in-person interaction.

stories.title: Multiple airstrikes hit Beirut, emergency crews respond to fires
stories.summary: Multiple Israeli strikes hit Beirut shortly after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced, sending smoke over the city.

stories.title: Camel calf Sophia born at Whipsnade Zoo, boosting conservation focus

stories.title: Watch: Fire damages roof of Rio's Olympic velodrome

stories.summary: In a televised speech, Netanyahu also hit back at opposition leaders who chastised him for agreeing to the truce before Israel achieved its objectives in the war.

stories.summary: Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but the LGBTQ+ community is frequently targeted by authorities who blame it for the country's declining birth rate.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran

stories.title: Prosecutors seek Tiger Woods' prescription drug records after Florida arrest

stories.summary: Rövid megbeszélést tartott az amerikai elnök és a főtitkár az iráni helyzetről, de a legégetőbb kérdésről Rutte nem nyilatkozott.

stories.summary: A volt nyomozó a HVG-nek adott interjújában azt mondta: politikusi pályára nem készül, de szívesen nyitna kutyanapközit, aminek lehet, hogy itt az ideje.

stories.summary: Nem ürít, égett szag a vécénél, és a ventilátor is elakadt.

stories.summary: Ezzel csökkentenék a fiatalok szorongását és alvási problémáit.

stories.title: Ellenőrzött frissesség, spórolás nagyban – ezt tudja az ALDI (x)
stories.summary: Minőségi és pénztárcakímélő vásárlás az ALDI-ban – frissességgaranciával és XXL kiszerelésekkel.

Akármilyen irányba is megy innen a helyzet, egy dologban szinte biztosak lehetünk: a konfliktus előtti szintre jó darabi

stories.title: Az iráni tűzszünet körüli bizonytalanság miatt újra kilőtt az olaj ára
stories.summary: A szerdai zuhanás után ismét emelkednek az árak, a részvényárfolyamok pedig az ázsiai és az európai tőzsdéken is esnek.

stories.title: Alig bírnak a turistákkal egy japán városban, elhalasztanak egy cseresznyevirágos fesztivált
stories.summary: A turisták új életet hoznak az elnéptelenedő japán településekbe, de sok helyi arra panaszkodik, hogy a külföldiek szemetelnek és nyilvános helyeken vizelnek.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin feljelentést tett választási csalás gyanúja miatt
stories.summary: A dunaújvárosi körzet fideszes jelöltjét, Mészáros Lajost szavazatvásárlással vádolja.

stories.title: Sátoraljaújhely fideszes polgármestere felszólította Wáberert, hogy mondjon le a tisztségeiről
stories.summary: Szamosvölgyi Péter szerint tokaji posztjairól kellene lemondania az üzletembernek politikai megnyilvánulásai miatt.

stories.title: Orosz tengeralattjárók egy hónapon át ügyködtek brit tengeri vezetékeknél
stories.summary: A brit védelmi miniszter szerint a szabotázsakció sikertelen volt, folyamatosan figyelték a három tengeralattjárót.

stories.title: Krasznahorkait, Tarr Bélát és Bereményit ünnepli a Margó Irodalmi Fesztivál
stories.summary: Április 9. és 11. között több mint harminc, zömmel ingyenes programot, könyvbemutatókat, beszélgetéseket, dedikálásokat és koncerteket tartanak a Margitszigeten. Mi ezeket várjuk a legjobban.

stories.title: Reuters: Egy új kisebb, olcsóbb SUV-on dolgozik a Tesla
stories.summary: Olcsóbb lenne, mint a Model 3, de az akkumulátor is kisebb lenne.

stories.title: Loptak a Szőlő utcai javítóintézet kiürítése közben, fekete zsákból kerültek elő a holmik
stories.summary: Egy bv-felügyelőnél és két rabnál találtak dolgokat, a rendőrség kisebb értékre elkövetett lopás gyanújával nyomoz.

stories.title: Beazonosították a tizedik Facebook-oldalt, ami szabálytalan hirdetésekkel segíti Tuzson Bence kampányát
stories.summary: A Political Capital szisztematikusan derítette fel a minisztert népszerűsítő hálózatot.

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.

stories.title: 'Only the president knows' what he will do on Iran, White House says in statement
stories.summary: Trump had threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran agreed a deal to end the war on Tuesday, saying he was "considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil."

stories.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv
stories.summary: A small group of anti-war protesters gathered outside the US embassy’s Tel Aviv office, calling for an end to the conflict before police moved to disperse the demonstration.

stories.title: Police destroy opium poppy fields in Pakistan
stories.summary: Pakistani police destroyed hectares of opium poppy fields in a tribal region near the Afghan border, as farmers said poverty leaves them few alternatives.

stories.title: Iranians form human chains on bridges amid tensions with US and Israel
stories.summary: Hundreds of people in Iran formed human chains on bridges after a government call to show support and protect infrastructure during heightened tensions.

stories.title: Five teens to go on trial in Sweden over attempted murder of Iranian dissident
stories.summary: Sweden’s security service Sapo says Iran has ramped up its targeting of dissidents since the start of the Iran war, accusing them of conscripting organised crime groups to carry out “acts of violence” on Swedish soil.

stories.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles after declaring South 'most hostile enemy'
stories.summary: The back-to-back launches came after Pyongyang made it clear that it had no intention of improving ties with Seoul, whose government steadfastly expressed its hopes of restoring long-dormant dialogue.

stories.title: Cameroon 'military contractors' killed in Russia-Ukraine war - BBC confirms leaked message
stories.summary: The government usually refrains from commenting on the participation of its citizens in the conflict.

stories.title: Tehran accelerates executions of political prisoners since start of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran executed 14 prisoners on political charges in three weeks since the war began on 28 February, including protesters and alleged opposition members amid a continued internet shutdown.

stories.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
stories.summary: Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain. This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power

stories.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
stories.summary: Two-week ceasefire comes after Trump spoke to Pakistan’s leaders, with China also believed to be exerting influence over Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday barely an hour before Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire, with Tehran agreeing to temporarily reopen the strait of Hormuz. Israel also agreed to the ceasefire,

stories.title: Europe cannot bet on a post-Trump US turning back to sanity | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. But it is a battle to the death. Tyranny will either break the spirit of the republic or be quelled by it. Since t

stories.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
stories.summary: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventua

stories.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
stories.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
stories.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

stories.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
stories.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

stories.title: How Pakistan brokered a US-Iran ceasefire, and what's next
stories.summary: The US, Israel and Iran have agreed to cease hostilities for two weeks after Islamabad mediated a temporary truce. The deal is a major achievement for Pakistan, but what comes next is likely to be even more complicated.

stories.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
stories.summary: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released by Iraqi militant group, official says
stories.summary: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.

stories.title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Hormuz strait as US deadline for deal looms
stories.summary: One-fifth of the world's oil typically passes through the strait and Iran's chokehold during the war has sent energy prices soaring around the world.

stories.title: Construction workers block roads in France over soaring fuel prices
stories.summary: Construction workers set up a roadblock on the Nantes ring road to protest rising fuel costs, saying the sharp increase is putting pressure on their businesses.

stories.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.title: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

stories.title: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller wins election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
stories.summary: He will serve out the rest of Marjorie Taylor Greene's term, which ends in January 2027.

stories.title: Rescued US airman treated in Germany after CIA located him in Iran by heartbeat
stories.summary: Using top-tier technology, the CIA was able to locate a US weapons systems officer in Iran after his fighter jet was downed. He is now being treated at the Landstuhl Regional Medical Centre (LRMC), the largest American military hospital outside the US.

stories.title: Thousands of Indigenous people march in Brasilia for land rights
stories.summary: Around 7,000 Indigenous people rallied in Brasilia against land grabs, mining and logging, pressing Lula to honour rights and protect the Amazon.

stories.title: Iranian attacks continue in the Gulf, raising questions about ceasefire
stories.summary: Euronews journalists in Dubai and Doha are reporting new attacks across the Gulf, rattling the short-lived relief about the US-Iran ceasefire announcement.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes rare visit to China in 'journey for peace'
stories.summary: Taiwan’s opposition leader makes historic six-day trip to Beijing to “boost dialogue and promote peace” ahead of a heavily anticipated summit between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.
